item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The impact of the final log was all but entirely neutered by the fact that the description had already specifically mentioned that the anomaly was known to do exactly what it did in the log. The rest of the article tries to survive entirely off of repeating what's essentially the same joke, and without an effective stinger to finish it off I was left disappointed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why aren't the Foundation taking care of the other male talking mannequin? And why aren't there a mention of amnestics used for the assaulted man and woman and security guards? I know it is probably obviously used, but the lack of the mention kinda makes this scip a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i was looking at this page, trying to figure out why i don't like this article very much. i have no problems with funny scips, and this isn't just a dangerous thing or a Thing What Kills You. but then i realized: there's just no real draw here. it's a mannequin that hates poor fashion choices, which is an alright concept, but nothing is really done with it. it's like if 682 just stopped at "angry invincible lizard" or 3008 just stopped at "infinite IKEA." this is funny enough to be a joke article, but not interesting enough to be a mainlist. even that We Are Number One meme scip was more interesting than this.
and then there's the issue of why only this mannequin was contained. are talking mannequins who are also mall employees commonplace, but they just don't comment on people's outfits? is it just this particular mall? where did she get the money? the retrieval log raises more questions than it answers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…What did the Foundation do ith the other mannequins that 2353 was conversing with?

…Where is her employee card?

…Why does she have one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted shamelessly at "no crocs". Its really campy, and I loved every bit of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not gonna lie. When I first saw that picture, I actually thought it was a real woman. But then I looked at it again, then read the article. Also, holy crap. This is almost too hilarious. Thank God she's never seen me in my everyday clothes, though.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second she looks at me, she will probably explode.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
#what'rethose
+1, Soul. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FOUND IT AGAIN.
Still love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found it clichéd, predictable, and not even funny. Also the grammar is clunky in places (e.g. there ought be a comma before 'commonly' in the description of jorts).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed pretty hard from the sheer absurdity. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't upvote because this article doesn't really interest me. Needs to be a -J, but it did give me a laugh.

No vote, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does being really funny excuse the lack of originality in an article?

Hell if I know.

If it was a -J I'd vote for it, as a mainlist article I'm hesitant. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love mainlisters that are funny enough to be -Js. But I didn't find this one very interesting…no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I came out to have a good time and I'm honestly feeling so attacked right now."
- SCP-2353.

…I'll just leave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see what's so funny?? It's a mannequin that gets mad when people don't dress fashionably :-X but is that supposed to be the joke? I'm confused…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was trying to put my finger on what felt off for me. Katiekawaii, you bring up a good point about speech patterns. I have no idea what region this SCP is from or what projected age it believes itself to be. This SCP is either a wannabe from the movie "Clueless" or an avid television watcher who loves "Project Runway" and "Drag Race".

I like the humor but the being itself feels false. Some of the most gentle people I know love fashion and invite me to Fashion Week, even if I wasn't so fashionable at the time. I love Bill Cunningham of the NY Times and his take on the trends of people who dare to strike out. The crux for me:This SCP wants people to conform; fashion demands you take chances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Soulless, bless your mind as it is truly a gift from the God's. I enjoyed this all the way though and it's probably one of my top best SCPs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, interesting. How about this: "Even that, like, totally kind guy working the register was looking at you sideways." Or the slight variation: "Even that, like, totally kind guy working the register was giving you the look." [The ", like," is optional but adds authenticity ^_~]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They're… not too good looking. I'm far from being a fashion person or an aesthete, but those shoes really rub me the wrong way: they look like some cheap sweatshop-made toy… in shoe form.

Not to knock on you or anyone else for liking them or enjoying wearing them, but I wouldn't wear them myself unless they were a gift from my currently non-existing children.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The problem here is that, for certain specific purposes, I need the skip to describe the person specifically as MALE and KIND. Do you have any suggestions for that? I really do appreciate you nipping at my tonal issues btw. I have no idea what I'm doing here, haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even that kind, poor fella cashiering the damn place was giving you a side-eye!

This line is in a totally different voice than the rest of the SCP's lines. You're clearly going for a valley girl kind of voice, but this line sounds more 50-year-old male detective from the '40s. As an actual girl typing from the actual valley, I can suggest some alternatives:
"Even that tragic cashier was giving you the look."
"Even the guy at the register was giving you the look, and he's a hot mess."
"Even the guy at the register was giving you the look, and he's wearing cargo pants."
"Even that emo cashier was giving you the look from under his sideways bangs."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted, but what's wrong with Crocs? I like wearing them. They feel nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's funny, but that's all it is, and I feel like calling the UN to report wearing white after Labor Day is just too silly, as is the mannequin's general personality. Oddly, I'm of the mind that I'd have no-voted were this a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too silly for the mainlist by my own reckoning, and the article isn't really groundbreaking or different enough to sell me on reconsidering that stance. It really dislikes unfashionable clothing, and then that joke is hammered home several times. Not my thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You upvoted it because it is amazing and awesome, the same reason I did.

Well done, Soulless, you have earned this +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love every word of this scp. It's just so fucking campy in a way I've never seen on this wiki. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't loving it, but the mannequin's reaction upon realising the woman is blind made me laugh, and that's just about worth an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something something Crocs with socks in a box

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did I upvote this? Why did I upvote this?

Soulless, I can't even…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was silly in a very funny way. I really enjoyed it.

Also I am the only one who read the mannequin's dialogue as an hysterical 90's sitcom character?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fell for this hook, line, and sinker.

I enjoyed it all the way through, though I wonder what happens when it actually does get a hold of the UN. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to thank LadyKatie, Lex1nat0r, and Metaphysician for the eyeballs.
